sources_exist.note: Identity and all four deterministic dataset_inputs match the assigned manifest and derived dataset exactly. Six distinct cited domains/pages resolve to the intended subject, but the exact CT Acquisitions dental-multiples URL used twice is inaccessible, so the buy and exit claims cannot be audited from the cited source. The ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag is resolved as benign: the listed A, B, M, S, and V stored-versus-recomputed differences are within the orchestrator tolerance.

figures_match_sources.note: Every one of the seven citation occurrences has a material support defect. Clerri supports 35% broad AI use but not the quoted ADA 47% tested/<11% embedded claim or the selected 12%; the EDR study supports 52% adoption in a 2012 respondent survey but not a 10-year diffusion interval; ADA supports all-dentist age bands but not 35% of owners age 60+ or a documented succession shortage; the tracker counts 21 active consolidators under criteria that expressly include founder-owned/public platforms, not 21 PE-backed platforms; the multiples page is inaccessible; and Dental Economics reports patient mix, not revenue mix.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The role-level automatable fractions are bounded and appropriately preserve licensed chairside work; pi_moat=0.55 is directionally plausible. However, t50_years=9 rests on an unsupported start point and an invented approximately 10-year analog, while current_adoption_pct=0.12 is described as a weighted midpoint between <11% and 35% even though 12% is neither their midpoint nor produced by a disclosed weighting. pi_dist=0.50 also relies on treating a patient-count payer mix as a revenue mix and understates evidence that insurers lowered reimbursements.

rubric_consistent.note: The role decomposition, no-proxy separation, t50 uncertainty disclosure, and owner-compensation caveat follow the rubric. B is nevertheless built from an unsupported 35% owner-age estimate, an unsubstantiated succession bonus, and a 21 count that is not limited to PE-backed add-on acquirers. M is built from inaccessible multiple evidence and a normalization narrative without auditable transaction support for 6.5x and 11.0x.

cross_checks_honest.note: The uplift check is internally candid and the DURABLE class is directionally sensible for licensed chairside care. But no_proxy_confirmed presents the <11% adoption and approximately 10-year EDR analog as established when the audited citations do not establish them. Pricing_model_deflation converts 57% insurance patients into 57% PPO revenue, adds a separately unsourced Medicaid share, and says savings largely remain with practices despite the cited survey reporting that 59% of dentists had at least one plan lower reimbursement in 2023. The terminal discussion also lacks the utilization evidence the prompt explicitly requires before inferring whether AI diagnostics changes treated volume.

source_audits.note: The tracker resolves and profiles 21 active platforms from January 2024 to May 2026, but its inclusion criteria expressly allow PE-backed, public-market-owned, or founder-owned consolidators. It identifies Pacific Dental Services as founder-owned with no PE and describes Aspen as primarily greenfield. It therefore does not support the record's claim that all 21 are PE-backed platforms with verified add-on acquisition activity.

The exact cited dental-multiples page is inaccessible. The auditable roll-up tracker contains directional practice ranges, but that is a different URL and does not validate the quoted FOCUS ranges, the approximately 30%-of-collections owner-compensation conversion, or 6.5x as a normalized-EBITDA median actually paid for the rubric's target band.

Clerri reports that 35% of practices have implemented some AI-powered technology, citing a secondary personal site. It contains neither the quoted ADA result that >47% tested AI but <11% embedded it nor support for 12% material adoption. Moreover, 12% is not the midpoint of 11% and 35%, and no weighting calculation is disclosed.

The exact cited dental-multiples page is inaccessible and cannot support the quoted 10.0x-15.0x platform band, the separately attributed FOCUS 9x-11x figure, or 11.0x as a conservative median for $10M-plus EBITDA platform exits.

The study reports self-reported current EDR use of 52% among 477 U.S. dentist respondents surveyed in 2012. It does not date broad availability to 2000-2005 or establish that adoption took approximately 10 years to reach 50%; indeed, among adopters it reports mixed go-live timing, with 40% more than six years earlier, 29% three-to-five years earlier, and 28% within two years.

ADA supports the 2024 all-dentist age distribution (18.7% age 55-64 and 15.5% age 65+) and average retirement age of 68.7. The page does not provide an age-60-plus share for practice owners, the claimed 48% versus 15% solo-practice comparison, the 16.1% DSO figure, the ownership trend, or evidence of a succession shortage. The score-bearing 35% owner estimate and bonus are therefore not supported by this URL.

The survey supports a typical mix of 57% insurance patients and 43% fee-for-service patients among general-dentist respondents; it does not report shares of revenue, split insurance into PPO versus Medicaid, or support the stated 30%-45% FFS revenue-per-patient premium. It also reports 59% had one or more plans lower reimbursements in 2023, which materially qualifies the record's capture narrative.

reasons: At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://clerri.com/blog/dental-practice-technology-adoption-statistics), the page does not contain the quoted ADA >47%-tested/<11%-embedded result and does not support 12% material adoption; the stated weighted-midpoint derivation is mathematically unspecified and 12% is not the midpoint of 11% and 35%. Re-run with an accessible primary ADA survey and a reproducible definition/calculation for material practice-level adoption.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5849439/), 52% EDR use in a 2012 respondent survey does not establish broad availability in 2000-2005 or an approximately 10-year time to 50%. Re-run with dated adoption observations or primary market-history evidence that supports both the starting date and 50% crossing used to derive t50_years.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://www.ada.org/resources/research/health-policy-institute/dentist-workforce), the page gives all-dentist age bands but not 35% of practice owners age 60+, and it does not document the asserted succession shortage. Re-run with owner-specific age evidence and direct evidence for the structured succession_shortage_documented flag; do not infer the bonus from DSO growth alone.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/dental-dso-pe-rollup-tracker-2026/), the tracker uses a broader active-consolidator definition and includes founder-owned/non-PE platforms such as Pacific Dental Services, so it does not support 21 PE-backed add-on acquirers. Re-run the count by applying the frozen PE-backing and last-24-month add-on tests to each named platform with auditable sponsor and transaction evidence.

At inputs.buy_mult.url and inputs.exit_mult.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/dental-dso-ma-multiples-2026/), the exact cited page is inaccessible and the record supplies no auditable transaction evidence for 6.5x or 11.0x after market-rate owner-dentist compensation. Re-run with accessible size-matched EBITDA transaction benchmarks, disclose the owner-compensation conversion inputs, and support the statistic actually used in M.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://www.dentaleconomics.com/practice/article/55016675/higher-production-and-much-needed-stability-the-2024-de-levin-group-annual-practice-survey), the source reports patient counts, not revenue shares, and does not support the PPO/Medicaid split used in pi_dist and pricing_model_deflation. Re-run with payer-specific revenue evidence and address the same source's finding that reimbursements were lowered for many practices when evaluating whether AI savings persist.

At cross_checks.terminal_value.justification, the record infers that AI diagnostics may increase treated volume without utilization evidence, contrary to the prompt's explicit terminal-value requirement. Re-run with evidence on visit/treatment utilization from augmented diagnostics or state the uncertainty without assuming direction.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, M, S, V
